source.multipart_basis: ASC Single-Occupant solar vehicles are most often built as a CFRP/hybrid monocoque with a bolted-in 4130 chromoly roll hoop and discrete subassemblies (seat pan, firewall, suspension pickups, solar-array deck, footwell). The single-body brief lumps the cage+tub into one welded structure; this multipart decomposition separates the load-bearing shell halves from the rollover cage and the localised pickups that actually carry suspension and solar-array reactions. Speculative at the level of shell lay-up subdivision; all load magnitudes, tube OD/wall, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved.

full_prompt:
Design the roll cage for a 2026 American Solar Challenge Single Occupant Class (SOC) vehicle, compliant with ASC 2026 Regulations Rev B. The cage surrounds a single upright driver; vehicle design mass with driver 350 kg. Required per ASC 2026 Rev B: primary roll hoop members 31.75 mm (1.25 in) OD x 2.0 mm wall (0.080 in) mild steel or 4130 chromoly tubing. Helmet clearance to any cage surface >= 50 mm; helmet top >= 50 mm below the top of the main roll hoop when the driver is secured. Material: 4130 chromoly normalized steel, E = 205 GPa, yield = 460 MPa, ultimate = 670 MPa, density = 7850 kg/m^3. Geometry: main hoop height 1300 mm, width at top 600 mm, wheelbase of vehicle 2500 mm (context). Cage anchors at 4 primary bolts M12 Grade 8.8 to the carbon chassis. Load cases per ASC 2026 Rev B regulations (derived from FIA-like rollover test): (R1) Vertical roof crush: 1.5 x vehicle mass x g = 1.5 x 350 x 9.81 = 5,150 N applied vertically at the top of the main hoop; max von Mises in tubes <= yield / 1.15 = 400 MPa; vertical deflection at load point <= 50 mm (maintain helmet clearance). (R2) Lateral load: 1.0 x vehicle mass x g = 3,433 N applied horizontally at the top of the main hoop; max deflection <= 50 mm; max von Mises <= 400 MPa. (R3) Longitudinal load: 0.6 x vehicle mass x g = 2,060 N at the top; max von Mises <= 400 MPa; max deflection <= 50 mm. (R4) Brake test: vehicle shall decelerate from 60 km/h (16.67 m/s) to rest with all 4 wheels locked. Brake-related loads are not transferred through the cage but the cage shall not interfere with brake-actuator packaging (geometric constraint). (R5) Cage mass <= 8 kg. (R6) Battery-energy cap per ASC 2026 Rev B Li-ion chemistry: pack energy <= 5.28 kWh (reference, non-structural). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static for R1-R3). Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded cage, weld map, and a report listing mass in kg, max deflection and max von Mises per load case.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The ASC SOC vehicle is delivered as a hybrid CFRP-monocoque + steel roll-hoop assembly, not a monolithic cage. Speculated families: (A) upper shell half (CFRP sandwich: canopy ring, rear fairing); (B) lower shell half (CFRP sandwich: floor pan with longitudinal hat stiffeners, wheel tubs); (C) main roll hoop (4130 chromoly 31.75 OD x 2.0 wall, 1300 mm tall, 600 mm wide at top -- the ONLY structure carrying LC1/LC2/LC3 roll-hoop loads to FEA rule-level); (D) seat pan / driver harness backplate (CFRP or Al sandwich, bonded to B); (E) firewall separating driver cell from battery/electronics (meets ASC Rev B 10.5 isolation); (F) suspension pickup inserts (4x front wishbone + 4x rear wishbone + 4x damper, Al 7075 bonded/bolted into B); (G) solar-array deck (non-structural composite panel bolted to A); (H) driver footwell / bulkhead (CFRP panel bonded to B, ties front of B to front pickups F). Interfaces: A<->B perimeter bonded + bolted secondary-bond flange; C<->B roll hoop anchors (4x M12 Grade 8.8 through-bolted into molded-in hardpoints -- the ASC primary anchor pattern); D<->B bonded to floor; E<->B bonded to rear bulkhead; F<->B bonded/bolted through local inserts; G<->A bolted (non-structural); H<->B bonded. FEM verification runs on the assembled model ONLY per fem_check_policy=assembled_only. The roll-hoop rule loads LC1/LC2/LC3 (5150/3433/2060 N at hoop top) are applied at C's top and load paths through C<->B (IF2) into the lower shell; shell per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part DCR <= 1.0 under the governing case (worst of LC1/LC2/LC3). The hoop-tube OD (31.75 mm), wall (2.0 mm), anchor bolt count/grade (4x M12 8.8), deflection limits (50 mm), stress limit (400 MPa), and mass cap (8 kg for the cage-portion only C) are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design the roll cage for a 2026 American Solar Challenge Single Occupant Class (SOC) vehicle as a hybrid CFRP-monocoque + steel roll-hoop multi-part assembly, compliant with ASC 2026 Regulations Rev B. Vehicle design mass with driver 350 kg; wheelbase 2500 mm.

Primary roll hoop per ASC 2026 Rev B: 31.75 mm (1.25 in) OD x 2.0 mm (0.080 in) wall 4130 chromoly normalized (E = 205 GPa, yield = 460 MPa, ultimate = 670 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). Main hoop height 1300 mm, width at top 600 mm. Helmet clearance to any cage surface >= 50 mm; helmet top >= 50 mm below main hoop top. Cage anchors at 4 primary bolts M12 Grade 8.8 to the carbon chassis.

LC1 vertical roof crush 1.5 x mass x g = 5150 N at hoop top; max deflection <= 50 mm; max von Mises <= yield / 1.15 = 400 MPa. LC2 lateral 1.0 x mass x g = 3433 N at hoop top; <= 50 mm and 400 MPa. LC3 longitudinal 0.6 x mass x g = 2060 N; <= 50 mm and 400 MPa. Cage mass <= 8 kg. Brake test: decelerate from 60 km/h (16.67 m/s) to rest with all 4 wheels locked. Pack energy <= 5.28 kWh Li-ion (reference).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: CFRP shell stresses are checked with laminate-level Tsai-Wu or max-strain criteria post-FEA; shell von Mises is not the governing metric for composites.

prompt.title: ASC 2026 SOC hybrid monocoque + 4130 roll cage (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: CFRP upper/lower shell monocoque with a bolted-in 4130 chromoly 31.75 OD x 2.0 wall main roll hoop; 1.5/1.0/0.6 g rollover/lateral/longitudinal test loads gated against 50 mm deflection at the hoop top. Multi-part: upper shell (A), lower shell (B), main roll hoop (C), seat pan (D), firewall (E), suspension pickup inserts (F), solar-array deck (G), footwell bulkhead (H). FEM assembled-only; connection DCR <= 1.0.

anchor_bolts.pattern_note: 4 primary anchors tie the main hoop C into molded hardpoints in the lower shell B

material.name: AISI 4130 normalized (hoop) + CFRP sandwich (shell)
material.cfrp_hint: Quasi-isotropic CFRP sandwich, E_in-plane ~55 GPa effective, honeycomb or foam core; checked by laminate analysis not von Mises
material.insert_material: Al 7075-T6 at F suspension pickups and C hoop anchor hardpoints

boundary_conditions.mounting: lower shell B supported at an idealized floor (distributed fixed over wheel-contact footprints) OR, when cage-only sub-model is analyzed, the 4 M12 anchor holes in B are fixed
boundary_conditions.loads: point or distributed at hoop top (rigid steel pad simulating the rollover test platen)
boundary_conditions.bonded_joints: A<->B perimeter bond modeled as tied surfaces; F inserts modeled with equivalent stiffness bond to B

output_format.body_count: multi-body (A + B + C + D + E + F x12 + G + H)

parts.name: upper_shell_half
parts.role: aerodynamic canopy fairing and upper surface carrying the solar array deck G; secondary structural tie at perimeter bond

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP sandwich with foam or honeycomb core

parts.name: lower_shell_half
parts.role: primary structural tub carrying the driver cell, the roll-hoop C anchor hardpoints, and all suspension pickup inserts F

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP sandwich with foam or honeycomb core + longitudinal hat stiffeners along the wheelbase

parts.name: main_roll_hoop
parts.role: primary rollover protection; carries LC1/LC2/LC3 loads per ASC Rev B rule

geometric_constraints.anchors: 4x M12 Grade 8.8 through-bolted to B hardpoints

parts.name: seat_pan
parts.role: driver seat pan and harness backplate; non-structural to the rollover check but bonded to B

parts.name: firewall
parts.role: driver-side isolation from battery/electronics (ASC Rev B isolation); structurally ties rear bulkhead of B

geometric_constraints.construction: CFRP sandwich or aluminum honeycomb panel
geometric_constraints.fire_rating: meets ASC Rev B isolation (separate gate)

parts.name: suspension_pickup_insert
parts.role: discrete hardpoint receiving wishbone/damper loads and feeding them into the lower shell B

parts.name: solar_array_deck
parts.role: non-structural composite panel carrying the solar array; bolted to A

parts.name: footwell_bulkhead
parts.role: forward driver-footwell bulkhead; ties front of B to front-suspension pickups F

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward, Y left-right, Z vertical
assembly_envelope.assembly_mass_hint_kg: vehicle design mass including driver = 350; structural mass budget allocated team-wide; hoop C alone capped at 8 kg

pass_fail_criteria.scope_note: applies to metallic parts C, F; CFRP parts use Tsai-Wu failure index <= 1.0 instead

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across all inter-part interfaces (IF1..IF8) <= 1.0 under the governing load case (worst of LC1/LC2/LC3).
Hoop-to-shell anchor bolts (IF2) shall not exceed utilization 1.0 under worst-case LC; governs the load path from the 4130 hoop into the CFRP tub.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (B32) for C tubes; shell (S4R) for shells A/B/D/E/G/H; solid for F inserts
connection_modeling: C-to-B anchors as bolt elements with preload and shear-tension check
A<->B perimeter bond as tied contact
F inserts as embedded solid with stiffness match

interfaces.mediated_by: perimeter structural adhesive bond + mechanical secondary-bond fasteners every ~150 mm
interfaces.load_path: upper-to-lower shear tie around canopy perimeter

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M12 Grade 8.8 bolts into Al 7075 hardpoints bonded into B
interfaces.load_path: PRIMARY rollover reaction: all of LC1/LC2/LC3 at hoop top flows through these 4 anchors into B
driver weight + harness reaction into floor
ties rear bulkhead stiffness; negligible cage-rule load

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded-in Al 7075 block with bolted wishbone/damper connection
interfaces.load_path: suspension reactions into floor; not part of cage-rule LC but present in full vehicle analysis
array weight and aerodynamic lift into upper shell; non-structural to cage rule
ties front of B to front-suspension pickups; small share of LC3 longitudinal
point of load application for LC1/LC2/LC3

fem_check_policy.rationale: The ASC rollover loads are applied at the main hoop top but react into the CFRP tub through 4 bonded hardpoints; isolated hoop-only FEA mis-estimates the anchor stiffness and thus the hoop deflection. Full-assembly FEA is required to gate the 50 mm deflection limit correctly.

notes.why_multi_part: ASC SOC vehicles are built as CFRP monocoques with bolted-in steel roll hoops; treating the whole thing as a welded tube assembly mis-represents the load path and the mass budget.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact shell subdivision (left/right halves, canopy ring separate) and pickup insert count are team-specific. Counts here reflect a plausible mid-complexity SOC vehicle; no additional ASC rulebook clauses are cited.
